mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and expanding economy, which typically translates into strong corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence rises.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth consistently fails to correlate positively with S&P 500 20-day returns over multiple economic cycles, or if the Rank IC is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and impending economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where the UNRATE trigger condition is met, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in both retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates broad-based economic expansion, signaling robust consumer demand and manufacturing activity, which collectively drives higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 3%
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO show strong growth as per the trigger, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat in the majority of cases (i.e., less than 55% positive returns).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) during a post-recession recovery period indicate renewed consumer and business confidence, stimulating construction, related industries, and broader economic activity, thereby supporting equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average > 850 (thousands of units) AND HOUST 3-month growth > 10%
failure_condition: During identified post-recession recovery periods, if rising HOUST does not lead to positive S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces borrowing costs for companies and consumers, encourages investment, and compresses discount rates, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% for at least 3 consecutive months
regime_hint: zero lower bound / accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.01%) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% for 3+ months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, leading to reduced corporate profit margins and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annualized, or if the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a strong and expanding economy, which translates directly into higher corporate earnings, increased investor confidence, and ultimately drives positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If periods of strong GDPC1 growth consistently coincide with negative or flat S&P 500 5-day returns (average return < 0.005%) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment (UNRATE) and strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a tightening and healthy labor market, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by > 0.2 percentage points over 3 months AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1.5%
failure_condition: When both UNRATE falls and PAYEMS grows as per the trigger, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in the majority of cases (i.e., less than 55% positive returns).
